ks1.objective: Dribble your ball, score in the goal, and watch out for the 'goal hunters'!
successCriteria: Keeps the ball close while dribbling
Aims a shot at the goal
Stays in role when tackled — becomes a goal hunter

lks2.objective: Combine dribbling with awareness — score in the goal while protecting the ball from the goal hunters.
successCriteria: Uses body to shield the ball
Picks the right moment to shoot
Joins the hunters cleanly when tackled

space.description: Square area with one coned goal at one end. Everyone inside the area to start.

steps: Everyone has a ball, except 2 children chosen as Goal Hunters.
Children with balls dribble around — keeping their ball — and try to score into the goal.
Goal Hunters aim to tackle other children and steal their ball.
If tackled, the child retrieves their ball after the goal hunter's shot, then continues.
Progress: when a child is tackled, they BECOME a goal hunter — game continues until all balls are gone.
progressions: Start with only 2 hunters — game ends when a few balls have been won.
Tackled players become hunters — game ends when all balls are taken.
Add a second goal to choose between.
Children must complete a turn before shooting at the goal.
questions: How do you protect the ball when a hunter is near?
What helps you find space to shoot?
What do you do straight after losing the ball?

step.space: Smaller area increases tackling pressure; larger area makes scoring easier
step.task: Children must perform a turn or skill before shooting
step.equipment: Two goals at different ends — more choices for the dribbler
step.people: Start with more hunters for a faster, more intense game

base.showmanship: Carefully select the 'goal hunters' so that you have a mix of abilities. Commentate on the game and celebrate the goals scored — narrate the moments of skill, bravery and resilience.
